summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude Code sessions.
summary.outcome: Successfully built a working VS Code extension with a sessions list, session detail tabs, native checkpoint tree view, diff integration, search, new/resume session terminals, onboarding flow, and VS Code theme integration â€” all backed by the existing gossamer server spawned as a child process.

repo: Entire.io writes session data to a specific git branch; gossamer indexes that branch into SQLite â€” the extension only needs to detect `.entire/settings.json` to know a repo is enabled

better-sqlite3 is a native module that must run on system Node.js; VS Code's extension host uses a different Electron-bundled Node.js (MODULE_VERSION mismatch), so Prisma/SQLite must be in a spawned child process not the extension host

The repo registry lives at `~/.gossamer/config.json` with `localPath`, `dbPath`, `name`, `remote` fields; `findRepo()` used exact match which breaks when cwd is a subdirectory â€” needed `findRepoForPath()` with prefix matching

The `/api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints` endpoint was returning null commitHash/gitOid; had to batch-fetch from `CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` table and run `git log --format=%s -1 <gitOid>` against candidate repos
repoName in session list was incorrectly showing `basename(repoDir)` (server's configured workspace) instead of `basename(session.cwd)` per-session
The `ShadowSession.cwd` is the source of truth for which git repo a checkpoint belongs to; without it, the fallback must try all configured repos in `~/.gossamer/config.json`
Schema push on server startup takes several seconds; the extension must poll `GET /api/sessions` until the server responds before signaling the webview

code.finding: Originally only listed `extension.ts` and `GossamerPanel.ts` in `include`, causing `SessionDetailPanel.ts` and `diffUtils.ts` to never be compiled â€” silent runtime failure. Must use `src/**/*.ts` with `src/webview` excluded.
`process.execPath` points to VS Code's bundled Electron node, not system node. Must use `execFileSync('which', ['node'])` to get system node for spawning the gossamer server.
Webview reloads when a tab is moved to a new window; `server_ready` must be re-sent via `onDidChangeViewState` using a `serverReady` boolean flag.

Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref is null at the `[]`-dep effect time, so a separate scroll listener never attaches. Fix: check scroll position inline (`scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight < 80`) at the moment new content arrives.

`root: 'src/webview'` is relative to CWD when run via npm workspace, not the package directory. Must use `resolve(__dirname, 'src/webview')` with `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` for ESM compatibility.
Vite outputs ESM; the `<script>` tag needs `type='module'` or the browser silently ignores it, causing a blank webview.
The `/api/v2/checkpoints/:id/file` endpoint must look up the session's cwd via `CheckpointSessionMetadata â†’ ShadowSession` to find the correct git repo, not use the server's global `repoDir`.
Added `findRepoForPath(cwd)` that finds a repo by prefix match â€” necessary because `cwd` can be a subdirectory of a registered repo root.

workflow: VS Code webview panels reload from scratch when dragged to a new window â€” any one-time setup (server readiness signals, injected globals) must be re-emitted on `onDidChangeViewState`

For native diffs without temp files, register a `TextDocumentContentProvider` with a custom URI scheme (`gossamer-diff:`), store content in memory, pass URIs to `vscode.diff` â€” files get syntax highlighting from their extension in the URI path
VS Code automatically injects `--vscode-*` CSS variables and `vscode-dark`/`vscode-light` body classes into webviews; combine with Mantine's `forceColorScheme` for full theme integration
`vscode.window.createQuickPick` is the idiomatic VS Code pattern for search â€” supports live updates, item groups with separators, and fuzzy matching
Context menu commands on TreeView items require declaring `menus['view/item/context']` entries in `package.json` with `when` clauses matching `viewItem` values set on tree items
Build order matters in npm workspaces: `packages/core` must be built before `packages/vscode` since the extension host imports from `@gossamer/core`

friction: Multiple context window truncations during a single session caused repeated re-reading of files and re-deriving of context

The `tsconfig.extension.json` bug (missing files from `include`) caused a completely silent failure â€” compiled JS imported non-existent modules at runtime with no error until the diff feature was tested
The content provider approach for diffs was implemented, then abandoned (URI encoding fragility), then re-adopted â€” two cycles of rework
Vite's deterministic output filenames required explicit `entryFileNames`/`assetFileNames` config; default hashed names broke the hardcoded reference in `GossamerPanel.ts`
Auto-scroll fix required three attempts: initial `useEffect` fix, then discovering the Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref was null at attach time, requiring an inline scroll position check
The `which node` approach to find system node is fragile on systems where `which` is not available or node is not on PATH
Commit message lookup for checkpoints required trying multiple candidate repo directories since `ShadowSession.cwd` is null for historically indexed sessions
open_items: Show session name instead of session ID in the sessions table when a session has been renamed (tabled by user)
The `which node` approach for finding system node may fail on some systems; consider making the node path configurable in extension settings
Onboarding panel only detects `.entire/settings.json` but doesn't verify the entire.io CLI is actually installed or the branch is configured
The gossamer server process lifecycle is tied to the GossamerPanel webview; if the panel is closed, the server dies and all SessionDetailPanels lose their data source
No cleanup of the in-memory `GossamerDiffProvider` content store â€” could grow unbounded in long sessions
Branch checkpoints via `/api/branch-log` require `findRepoForPath` to find the right repo, but if multiple repos share a common path prefix the wrong one could be selected
The `POST /api/repos` registration on first open kicks off background indexing but there is no progress feedback to the user while initial indexing runs
Optional future: package `packages/core` as a standalone CLI tool (`gossamer-index`)
